close_reason: Replaced matches\! with SDK-provided is_not_found_exception method references
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:235 and :287 use:
  if matches!(svc, SignError::NotFoundException(_)) { ... }
  if matches!(svc, GetPublicKeyError::NotFoundException(_)) { ... }

The aws-sdk-kms 1.105 SignError and GetPublicKeyError types both expose 'is_not_found_exception() -> bool' helper methods, which are what sister crate secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:94 uses ('svc.is_resource_not_found_exception()').

What goes wrong: matches! on a non_exhaustive enum variant is brittle if the SDK upgrades and renames the variant — generated 'is_*' helpers track the rename. matches! requires importing SignError and GetPublicKeyError; the helper does not, removing two unused imports.

Fix: replace both matches!() with svc.is_not_found_exception(). Removes 'use aws_sdk_kms::operation::sign::SignError' and 'use aws_sdk_kms::operation::get_public_key::GetPublicKeyError' imports at lines 32-33.

Sub-finding: this also enables F2's helper extraction (the helper would be generic over an error type with an is_not_found_exception() method, which a tiny trait or a thin macro can express).
title: Use is_not_found_exception() helper instead of matches!(SignError::NotFoundException(_))
